title: buildah chokes on dockerfile syntax
body:
```
2018-10-02T19:02:14.575036033Z Storing signatures
2018-10-02T19:02:46.852178787Z Generating dockerfile with builder image docker.io/bucharestgold/centos7-s2i-nodejs@sha256:a718a9daf8074aacd08e2de2679c067b39790f8e89e860f31d03e8fadd2d424a
2018-10-02T19:02:46.864097324Z Using buildah to create image temp.builder.openshift.io/e2e-test-prometheus-gj9m5/frontend-1:a8f3ad53
2018-10-02T19:02:46.864461829Z STEP 1: FROM docker.io/bucharestgold/centos7-s2i-nodejs@sha256:a718a9daf8074aacd08e2de2679c067b39790f8e89e860f31d03e8fadd2d424a
2018-10-02T19:02:46.886543093Z STEP 2: LABEL "io.openshift.build.image"="docker.io/bucharestgold/centos7-s2i-nodejs@sha256:a718a9daf8074aacd08e2de2679c067b39790f8e89e860f31d03e8fadd2d424a"       "io.openshift.build.commit.author"="Honza Horak <hhorak@redhat.com>"       "io.openshift.build.commit.date"="Tue Sep 25 07:47:27 2018 +0200"       "io.openshift.build.commit.id"="905416d88f74bfb734efac2a7e835948128a1df1"       "io.openshift.build.commit.ref"="master"       "io.openshift.build.commit.message"="Merge pull request #204 from jeffdyoung/ppc64le"       "io.openshift.build.source-location"="https://github.com/openshift/nodejs-ex.git"
2018-10-02T19:02:46.886662541Z STEP 3: ENV OPENSHIFT_BUILD_NAME="frontend-1"     OPENSHIFT_BUILD_NAMESPACE="e2e-test-prometheus-gj9m5"     OPENSHIFT_BUILD_SOURCE="https://github.com/openshift/nodejs-ex.git"     OPENSHIFT_BUILD_COMMIT="905416d88f74bfb734efac2a7e835948128a1df1"
2018-10-02T19:02:46.886673744Z STEP 4: USER root
2018-10-02T19:02:46.886677656Z STEP 5: COPY upload/src /tmp/src
2018-10-02T19:02:47.274455003Z STEP 6: RUN chown -R 1001:0 /tmp/src
2018-10-02T19:02:47.709409449Z Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Invalid argument
2018-10-02T19:02:47.78101073Z error: build error: error building at step {Env:[OPENSHIFT_BUILD_COMMIT=905416d88f74bfb734efac2a7e835948128a1df1 OPENSHIFT_BUILD_SOURCE=https://github.com/openshift/nodejs-ex.git OPENSHIFT_BUILD_NAMESPACE=e2e-test-prometheus-gj9m5 OPENSHIFT_BUILD_NAME=frontend-1 PATH=/opt/app-root/src/node_modules/.bin/:/opt/app-root/src/.npm-global/bin/:/opt/app-root/src/bin:/opt/app-root/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin SUMMARY=Platform for building and running Node.js 10.11.0 applications DESCRIPTION=Node.js  available as docker container is a base platform for building and running various Node.js  applications and frameworks. Node.js is a platform built on Chrome's JavaScript runtime for easily building fast, scalable network applications. Node.js uses an event-driven, non-blocking I/O model that makes it lightweight and efficient, perfect for data-intensive real-time applications that run across distributed devices. STI_SCRIPTS_URL=image:///usr/libexec/s2i STI_SCRIPTS_PATH=/usr/libexec/s2i APP_ROOT=/opt/app-root HOME=/opt/app-root/src BASH_ENV=/opt/app-root/etc/scl_enable ENV=/opt/app-root/etc/scl_enable PROMPT_COMMAND=. /opt/app-root/etc/scl_enable NODEJS_SCL=rh-nodejs8 NPM_RUN=start NODE_VERSION=10.11.0 NPM_VERSION=6.4.1 NODE_LTS=false NPM_CONFIG_LOGLEVEL=info NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX=/opt/app-root/src/.npm-global NPM_CONFIG_TARBALL=/usr/share/node/node-v10.11.0-headers.tar.gz DEBUG_PORT=5858     OPENSHIFT_BUILD_NAME=frontend-1 OPENSHIFT_BUILD_NAMESPACE=e2e-test-prometheus-gj9m5 OPENSHIFT_BUILD_SOURCE=https://github.com/openshift/nodejs-ex.git OPENSHIFT_BUILD_COMMIT=905416d88f74bfb734efac2a7e835948128a1df1] Command:run Args:[chown -R 1001:0 /tmp/src] Flags:[] Attrs:map[] Message:RUN chown -R 1001:0 /tmp/src Original:RUN chown -R 1001:0 /tmp/src}: error parsing PID "": strconv.Atoi: parsing "": invalid syntax
```

Looks like it does not like `RUN chown -R 1001:0 /tmp/src`

comments.body: Thanks @bparees .  Do you have the Dockerfile handy that you could drop into a reply?  FYI @giuseppe
you can see the dockerfile in the log (each step of it is output).

I put another log in https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1061

i think they are the same issue, in both causes the error is really the unshare failure, i guess.

here's a failure that does not involve chown, but otherwise looks identical:

```
2018-10-02T18:59:36.793276002Z STEP 1: FROM centos/s2i-base-centos7
2018-10-02T18:59:36.907550928Z STEP 2: EXPOSE 8080
2018-10-02T18:59:36.908731779Z STEP 3: ENV RUBY_VERSION=2.3
2018-10-02T18:59:36.909087471Z STEP 4: ENV SUMMARY="Platform for building and running Ruby $RUBY_VERSION applications" DESCRIPTION="Ruby $RUBY_VERSION available as container is a base platform for building and running various Ruby $RUBY_VERSION applications and frameworks. Ruby is the interpreted scripting language for quick and easy object-oriented programming. It has many features to process text files and to do system management tasks (as in Perl). It is simple, straight-forward, and extensible."
2018-10-02T18:59:36.909377333Z STEP 5: LABEL summary="$SUMMARY" description="$DESCRIPTION" io.k8s.description="$DESCRIPTION" io.k8s.display-name="Ruby 2.3" io.openshift.expose-services="8080:http" io.openshift.tags="builder,ruby,ruby23,rh-ruby23" com.redhat.component="rh-ruby23-container" name="centos/ruby-23-centos7" version="2.3" usage="s2i build https://github.com/sclorg/s2i-ruby-container.git --context-dir=2.4/test/puma-test-app/ centos/ruby-23-centos7 ruby-sample-app" maintainer="SoftwareCollections.org <sclorg@redhat.com>"
2018-10-02T18:59:36.909466044Z STEP 6: RUN yum install -y centos-release-scl &&     INSTALL_PKGS="rh-ruby23 rh-ruby23-ruby-devel rh-ruby23-rubygem-rake rh-ruby23-rubygem-bundler" &&     yum install -y --setopt=tsflags=nodocs $INSTALL_PKGS && rpm -V $INSTALL_PKGS &&     yum clean all -y
2018-10-02T18:59:38.332109933Z Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Invalid argument
2018-10-02T18:59:39.96372049Z error: build error: error building at step {Env:[DESCRIPTION=Ruby 2.3 available as container is a base platform for building and running various Ruby 2.3 applications and frameworks. Ruby is the interpreted scripting language for quick and easy object-oriented programming. It has many features to process text files and to do system management tasks (as in Perl). It is simple, straight-forward, and extensible. SUMMARY=Platform for building and running Ruby 2.3 applications RUBY_VERSION=2.3 PATH=/opt/app-root/src/bin:/opt/app-root/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin SUMMARY=Base image with essential libraries and tools used as a base for builder images like perl, python, ruby, etc. DESCRIPTION=The s2i-base image, being built upon s2i-core, provides any images layered on top of it with all the tools needed to use source-to-image functionality. Additionally, s2i-base also contains various libraries needed for it to serve as a base for other builder images, like s2i-python or s2i-ruby. STI_SCRIPTS_URL=image:///usr/libexec/s2i STI_SCRIPTS_PATH=/usr/libexec/s2i APP_ROOT=/opt/app-root HOME=/opt/app-root/src BASH_ENV=/opt/app-root/etc/scl_enable ENV=/opt/app-root/etc/scl_enable PROMPT_COMMAND=. /opt/app-root/etc/scl_enable NODEJS_SCL=rh-nodejs8    RUBY_VERSION=2.3 SUMMARY=Platform for building and running Ruby 2.3 applications DESCRIPTION=Ruby 2.3 available as container is a base platform for building and running various Ruby 2.3 applications and frameworks. Ruby is the interpreted scripting language for quick and easy object-oriented programming. It has many features to process text files and to do system management tasks (as in Perl). It is simple, straight-forward, and extensible.] Command:run Args:[yum install -y centos-release-scl &&     INSTALL_PKGS="rh-ruby23 rh-ruby23-ruby-devel rh-ruby23-rubygem-rake rh-ruby23-rubygem-bundler" &&     yum install -y --setopt=tsflags=nodocs $INSTALL_PKGS && rpm -V $INSTALL_PKGS &&     yum clean all -y] Flags:[] Attrs:map[] Message:RUN yum install -y centos-release-scl &&     INSTALL_PKGS="rh-ruby23 rh-ruby23-ruby-devel rh-ruby23-rubygem-rake rh-ruby23-rubygem-bundler" &&     yum install -y --setopt=tsflags=nodocs $INSTALL_PKGS && rpm -V $INSTALL_PKGS &&     yum clean all -y Original:RUN yum install -y centos-release-scl &&     INSTALL_PKGS="rh-ruby23 rh-ruby23-ruby-devel rh-ruby23-rubygem-rake rh-ruby23-rubygem-bundler" &&     yum install -y --setopt=tsflags=nodocs $INSTALL_PKGS && rpm -V $INSTALL_PKGS &&     yum clean all -y}: error parsing PID "": strconv.Atoi: parsing "": invalid syntax
```

Yes I can grab it from the log, but I was hoping to not have to edit from the log to recreate it and also to get a hold of any other commands that were below the failure in case there were other problems later on.

@TomSweeneyRedHat if i can get a clean local recreate i'll provide the dockerfile, right now these failures are coming out of our CI system so it's a little messy to ensure i'm giving you exactly what it ran.
Thx for the 411 @bparees, I was thinking you had the file easily on hand, didn't realize it was buried away in the CI system.  If you can get it great, if not no sweat.
This looks like the unshare command is blocked.  Are we running in a container without SYS_ADMIN? If yes then it needs to be run with --isolation choot, I believe.
@bparees  add `--isolation chroot` or `export BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot` should do it for you.
discussed with @nalind on irc, we're already running isolation chroot
At the API level, setting `Isolation` to `IsolationChroot` in the applicable `BuilderOptions` or `BuildOptions` or `RunOptions` should also do that.

@bparees I've got a similar issue on centos 7
If you're on centos add `namespace.unpriv_enable=1 user_namespace.enable=1` to your kernel parameters.  

Or run `grubby --args="namespace.unpriv_enable=1 user_namespace.enable=1" --update-kernel="$(grubby --default-kernel)"` and add  `echo "user.max_user_namespaces=15000" >> /etc/sysctl.conf`

Then also run buildah with the `--isolation chroot`
we seem to have gotten past this in our tests, i'm not sure what the underlying fix/issue was, but i'm going to close it.
